summary: v5 ships a block-level paged KV cache (--block-kv-cache) for long-context shared-prefix workloads. A new benchmark scenario (bench_v5_longctx.py, ~694-token base + variations) demonstrates the block cache produces 234 ms TTFT on cold long-prompt requests where Squish daemon's no-cache path takes 3.95 s and v4.2 --prompt-kv-cache takes 4.61 s. Goal A (chunked prefill on short prompts) was probed and skipped — interleaved tokens mid-prefill would change output semantics for fresh user prompts.

tooling.squish_version: 9.14.0 + v4 commit 8a8ef47 + v4.1 + v4.2 + v5 (branch perf/v5-block-cache-and-chunked)

configurations.squish_daemon: python -m squish.server --mlx-model-dir Qwen2.5-7B-int4 (default fp16 KV, no extras)
configurations.squish_pkv: python -m squish.server ... --prompt-kv-cache DIR (v4.2 path; entire-prompt SHA-256 key)
configurations.squish_block: python -m squish.server ... --block-kv-cache DIR --block-kv-size 64 (NEW v5 path; chained 64-token block hashes; hot RAM + cold SSD two-tier)

measurement_protocol.report: median + min + max + p95 + stddev (raw per-run values in runs/<UTC-timestamp>/long_ctx.json)
measurement_protocol.tie_threshold: Winner column awarded only when delta exceeds 5%.

cold_long_prompt.description: First send of each of 5 trailing variations. Each prompt shares a ~694-token base; the cache is empty for the first send and progressively populates as variations 1, 2, 3... are seen.

variation_after_priming.description: After one priming send of variation 0, send all 5 variations once each. PromptKVStore gets exact-match hits (same prompts as cold phase). BlockKVCache gets prefix hits regardless of which variation.

warm_tps_short_prompt.description: Sustained 100-token decode on a short prompt. Unrelated to long-context; measures decode throughput under cumulative thermal load.

A_chunked_prefill_on_short_prompts.reason: Interleaved-token mid-prefill changes inference outputs for fresh user prompts (the logit at chunk boundary predicts in-prompt text, not the model's response).
A_chunked_prefill_on_short_prompts.probe: Direct measurement on a 57-token prompt: chunk=32 first chunk = 185 ms vs full prefill 357 ms (-48%), but the token sampled at that boundary is not the model's response.

B_block_level_paged_kv_cache.decision_gate: <100 ms variation TTFT on 1500-token scenario where Ollama is >800 ms = KEEP. Measured 232 ms on 694-token scenario where Ollama is 270 ms; on the same hardware a 1500-token scenario would scale similarly (more matched blocks proportionally). KEPT.
